name: JAK inhibition reduced SASP and improved aged-mouse physical function
summary: JAK-pathway inhibition is the strongest non-rapalog preclinical senomorphic function signal in this baseline.
statement: JAK inhibition suppressed SASP in senescent human primary preadipocytes and HUVECs, reduced the proinflammatory effect of conditioned media, and in aged mice treated for 10 weeks alleviated adipose and systemic inflammation while enhancing physical function.
population_or_model: Senescent human cell systems and aged mice
time_horizon: Ten weeks of JAK inhibitor treatment in aged mice
quantitative_note: The PubMed abstract describes aged-mouse treatment for 10 weeks and improved physical function, but does not provide a single effect size in the abstract.
caveats: The functional evidence is mouse data, not a human aging trial.
JAK inhibition has immune and infection-risk considerations that matter for preventive geroscience use.
The source supports senomorphic plausibility but not clinical efficacy for aging.
